frameworks.name: attractor neural network
frameworks.one_line: recurrent network where memories are basins of attraction subject to consolidation

frameworks.name: Hebbian learning with synaptic decay
frameworks.one_line: classical Hebbian encoding extended by decay and stochastic rehearsal

frameworks.name: memory consolidation theory
frameworks.one_line: novel stochastic-rehearsal mechanism with rates proportional to basin size

frameworks.name: catastrophic forgetting in continual learning
frameworks.one_line: computational problem of interference between sequentially encoded memories

core_question: How can a recurrent neural network continually store new memories over a lifetime without catastrophic forgetting?
key_assumptions: memories form basins of attraction in a recurrent network
rehearsal rate scales with basin of attraction size
manipulations: stochastic rehearsal rates proportional to basin size
synaptic decay time relative to memory lifetime
circuit perturbations to mimic neurological trauma
number of neurons in the network
key_findings: memory lifetimes extend much longer than the synaptic decay time
retrieval probability gracefully decays with memory age
number of retrievable memories scales as a power of neuron count
perturbations produce temporally-graded retrograde and anterograde deficits
interpretations: self-amplified stochastic consolidation enables lifelong memory in recurrent circuits
the model captures empirically observed memory impairments following trauma
